title: Access policy for benign and malicious users
prompt: A digital service sets one access-friction level before users choose whether to participate. Benign and malicious users respond strategically and have different private payoffs. Frame deterrence versus participation without treating user type as observed or claiming all friction improves safety.

model_specification.model_type: strategic participation model with private types
model_specification.mathematical_objective: maximize platform payoff from benign participation net of malicious harm
model_specification.mechanism: Friction changes participation incentives differently across latent user types.

prohibited_inferences: The service identifies each malicious user before access
